tweets.text: The future is accelerating

quoted.text:
"a first step towards becoming a Kardashev II-level civilization." - Elon Musk

In the last three weeks:

SpaceX acquired xAI, merging the world's largest rocket company with one of the fastest-moving AI labs on the planet. SpaceX valued at $1 trillion.

The stated goal of the merger: build orbital data centers. A constellation of a million satellites that generate AI compute in space, powered by near-constant solar energy with near-zero operating costs. Elon Musk's words: "Within 2 to 3 years, the lowest cost way to generate AI compute will be in space."

The math he laid out: launching a million tons per year of satellites generating 100 kW of compute per ton adds 100 gigawatts of AI compute capacity annually. The long-term path is 1 terawatt per year from Earth launches alone. And with lunar factories using electromagnetic mass drivers, 500 to 1,000 terawatts per year into deep space.

Elon Musk also announced SpaceX is building a self-growing city on the Moon. Target: under 10 years. First uncrewed landing: March 2027. Lunar manufacturing will feed the orbital compute network. Factories on the Moon building satellites and launching them deeper into the solar system.

And the rocket that makes all of it possible, Starship V3, with 100+ tons to orbit, orbital refueling, and Raptor 3 engines, is targeting its first flight in mid-March. The plan: launches every hour, 200 tons per flight, millions of tons to orbit per year.

The most powerful rocket in history. Aimed at the Moon. Designed to launch the largest AI infrastructure ever built. Weeks from flying.

It's happening.

quoted.text:
REID HOFFMAN'S PUBLIC CLAIMS VERSUS THE DOCUMENTS

Reid Hoffman and Jeffrey Epstein were not just associates.

They were best friends.

The evidence is overwhelming, and I constructed timelines proving it.

In the attached timelines, I document each event, scoring it from 1 to 5, with 1 being planned and 5 being confirmed. Most events are confirmed. The size of the bubble indicates the number of documents establishing the event.

In the attached PDFs below, I thoroughly document how the existence of each of these events was established, with verifiable proof from the available public evidence on the Department of Justice website.

Over 6 years, Epstein and Hoffman met at least 42 times and exchanged gifts and favors at least 44.

The spending ratio tells the story: Epstein showered Hoffman with private aviation, island access, and financial advice worth potentially hundreds of millions. Hoffman gave back the thing Epstein needed most: legitimacy and access to Silicon Valley's elite.

Let's go through Hoffman's claims.

CLAIM 1: "My few interactions" (Sep 12, 2019 - Axios)

Hoffman said: "My few interactions with Jeffrey Epstein came at the request of Joi Ito, for the purposes of fundraising for the MIT Media Lab."

REALITY: 42 documented meetings over 6 years. Gifts, Skype calls, three island trips, ranch visit, private jet use, $100M+ financial advisory relationship. Joi Ito introduced them -- but the relationship exploded far beyond "fundraising for MIT."

CLAIM 2: "Deeply regretful" (Sep 13, 2019 - Axios)

Hoffman said: "By agreeing to participate in any fundraising activity where Epstein was present, I helped to repair his reputation and perpetuate injustice. For this, I am deeply regretful."

REALITY: Hoffman didn't just "participate" -- he hosted dinners, flew on Epstein's jet, sent gifts "for the girls," stayed on the island three times, and personally brokered introductions between Epstein and Zuckerberg and others.

CLAIM 3: "Last interaction was in 2015" (Sep 2019 - Axios)

Hoffman's statement said his "last interaction with Epstein was in 2015."

REALITY: Documents show meetings/calls in April 2016 (Fuki Sushi), June 2016 (Prince Andrew lunch), July 2016 (Ito's house), January 2017 (Signal Exchange), November 2017 (breakfast + office), February 2018 (Bannon same day), March 2018 (60-90 min Skype), April 2018 (Secrecy Protocol breakfast), and August 2018 (BrainMind).

At least 15 documented contacts after 2015. All shown in these timelines and proven in the PDFs linked below.

CLAIM 4: "It gnaws at me" (2023 - Wall Street Journal)

Hoffman said: "It gnaws at me that, by lending my association, I helped his reputation, and thus delayed justice for his survivors."

REALITY: "Lending my association" frames Hoffman as passive -- a name on a guest list. The documents show the opposite: he hosted a Michelin restaurant buyout seating Epstein next to Zuckerberg and Musk, brokered the Zuckerberg-Epstein introduction through his own assistant, flew on Epstein's private jet three times to Little St. James, sent Christmas gifts "for the girls" on the island, and was still meeting Epstein in April 2018 with an active secrecy protocol -- a full year after this "gnawing" supposedly began. He didn't lend his association. He invested it, repeatedly, for 6 years. He even offered to help Epstein with PR when the public scandal started to explode.

CLAIM 5: "Only knew Epstein because of fundraising for MIT" (Nov 14, 2025 - X)

Hoffman: "I was never a client of Epstein's and never had any engagement with him other than fundraising for MIT."

REALITY: The gift dossier documents 44 exchanges including private aviation, estate/tax planning on $100-300M, crypto investments, Zorro Ranch weekends, and introductions to Prince Andrew, Xi Jinping contacts, and Saudi crown prince. None of this is "fundraising for MIT."

CLAIM 6: "I was mistaken" (Feb 4, 2026 - X)

Hoffman said: "In 2019 I told Axios the last meeting I had with him was in 2015 but I was mistaken, as according to calendar entries I have become aware there were additional fundraising meetings in 2016 and 2018."

REALITY: He disclosed "three meetings and three Skype calls" after 2015. We have 15+ documented meetings in 2016-2018 alone. Not just messaging. Actual meetings. Skype video calls and in-person visits.

CLAIM 7: "Did not go to the ranch" (Feb 3, 2026 - X)

Hoffman said: "I did not go to the ranch."

REALITY: Hoffman's visit to Zorro Ranch, March 19, 2014) is thoroughly documented with scheduling emails and travel logistics.

CLAIM 8: "I deeply regret" (Feb 2026 - X)

Hoffman said: "Yes, interacting with Epstein post-conviction is something I deeply regret. I promise to continue calling for the full release of the Epstein files -- the victims deserve justice, and those responsible should be held accountable."

REALITY: "Interacting" is doing extraordinary work here. 42 documented meetings. 44 gift exchanges. Three island trips. A ranch weekend. $100-300M in financial advisory services. Private jets. A Zuckerberg introduction. A secrecy protocol for concealing the relationship. And the phrase "those responsible should be held accountable" -- from a man whose own documents show he sent gifts "for the girls" to a convicted sex offender's private island, then helped that sex offender meet the CEO of Facebook.

tweets.text: RT @socraticexp: If your child becomes a reader, about 80% of the educational job is already done. That's our honest assessment, based on m…

retweet.text:
If your child becomes a reader, about 80% of the educational job is already done. That's our honest assessment, based on many of us working in education for over three decades. Everything else is secondary. Most parents think science education is important. Yes, it is. But if you can't read the biology textbook, you're not going to learn biology.

Reading is the meta-skill that enables all other skills. History requires reading. Science requires reading. Even math increasingly requires reading as it becomes more sophisticated. The child who reads voraciously will figure out everything else. The child who doesn't will struggle with everything.

> in 14 states, you don't have to show **any ID at all** to vote in federal elections

> requiring ID to vote is "Jim Crow 2.0" according to Schumer and other politicians

> but to shovel snow in NYC you must show 2 forms of ID + social security card

This is an IQ test for our civilization

It's paramount that we see through manipulative and  highly idiotic rhetoric and pass the SAVE America Act.

Proving citizenship to vote is one of the most popular policies in history (84% support across both parties).
